Utility Room (p. 22) 23. Mall Rats Clubhouse (p. 22) 24. The Bunker (p. 23) [30] Fallouts // The weirder you get, the more others may think you have been REPLACED. CUSTOM FALLOUT These new fallouts reinforce the mall’s "weird" happenings. They can be used instead of or in addition to the core Fallout Table in LH Investigators. ▶When a player fails a CTRL save due to stress, give them a Whisper Card (p. 37) and roll or choose a Fallout. ▶Keep the actual Fallout results secret from other players. d8FALLOUT TABLE 01 You hear the choir's song, its melody is an ever-changing constant. ▶Increase your CTRL by 1d4. 02 Rough patches have formed on your body—light red, surrounding a deep green. Are they getting bigger? ▶Increase your HP by d4. 03 Your insides feel like they are shifting, changing. Bones and joints start to move in ways they couldn't before. The changes are concealed by your clothing... for now. ▶Increase your STR by 1d6. 04 Thoughts and feelings become a thing you can sense, even when not in their proximity. Focus, hard, and words and phrases will enter your mind (onlooking PCs gain 1 Stress). ▶The more you focus on others, the more you lose yourself. 05 You continually see a figure out of the corner of your eye. It regularly shifts and warps, sometimes in horrific ways. You think there's a pattern to the change. 06 Piercing vision. With enough concentration you can see echoes of possible futures (onlooking PCs gain 1 Stress). Doing so changes your eyes. ▶Once per session have the Facilitator tell you a vision. 07 Sounds begin to manifest visually. At first, they are just shapes and colors, but they slowly start to become more distinct. Their form is unlike anything you've ever seen. 08Roll on the Liminal Horror Investigators Fallout Table. Nightmares // Everyone needs to sleep eventually. Proximity to the Portal means it offers little respite. [31] NIGHTMARES Whenever characters sleep, have them make a CTRL save. ▶ Success: The player cannot remember their dreams. ▶ Failure: roll d12 on the nightmare table: d12NIGHTMARE TABLE 01 Something moves underneath your skin, if only you could get a hold of it. 02 You stand before a bubbling portal. A chorus of voices is calling you home. 03 You are surrounded on all sides by stone and dirt, allowing only a small bit of movement. Something sharp starts biting into your feet. 04 The scent of cooking flesh makes your mouth water. It is time to tend the meat on the spit. You feel a small pain where your arm once was. 05 Their smile brings a warmth to your chest as they interlace their fingers in yours. Flames embrace you both. A pool of rendered fat sizzles at your feet. 06Limbs erupt out of your back and begin to pull you apart. 07 A pile of pink lays on the table before you. One more strip of flesh left until it is complete. 08He reaches within you. Bones snap as he builds something new. 09 It is almost finished growing within you. You are running out of time to make it home. 10 Fungus erupts from your every opening. You try to scream, but only yellow spores spill out. 11 As you take another bite, thick viscous fluid spills down your chin. Each mouthful catches in your throat as it wriggles its way deep within you. 12 You are falling deep within the earth, mud and stone packed tight around you gives way to pulsing flesh and bone. Finally you land in front of a massive beating heart that unfurls itself, exposing an unending abyss full of stars. [32] Character Creation // Using this process makes characters with ties to the mall and its denizens. Alternative character creation rules for The Mall, for use in place of Liminal Horror Investigators: Creating an Investigator. ▶A digital version can be found on the Liminal Horror Websiteor here: Each person at the table will propose one shop to be added to the mall (this is not necessarily their PC’s place of work). ▶State its name. ▶Description. ▶Floor (1-3). STEP 1: ADDING TO THE MALL CHARACTER CREATION: THE MALL Roll 3d6 for each of your ability scores (any two results may be swapped): ▶ Strength (STR): Physicality, brawn, toughness ▶ Dexterity (DEX): Speed, sneaking, precision ▶ Control (CTRL): Willpower, charm, weird Roll 1d6 for Hit Protection (HP). STEP 2: ABILITIES Answer the following questions: STEP 3: CHARACTER DETAILS WHY ARE YOU AT THE MALL? (likely reasons) You work at the mall. Where do you work? (p. 5) You
